description: Award winning Canadian DJ/Producer Felix Cartal returns to Upstairs for his album release tour "I sabotage". Felix Cartal is a prolific producer and DJ that hails from Vancouver, Canada. His style is energetic and melodic, and his shows match. His resume is vast, including collabs with Kaskade, Elohim, and Daya, as well as remixes for The Chainsmokers, Selena Gomez, Zedd, Illenium, and nimino’s hit “I Only Smoke When I Drink”... to name a few. Felix has racked up a string of hits, and won 2 JUNO Awards for Best Dance Recording (2020’s “Love Me” and 2024’s “Need Your Love”). Recently, he released his latest album, i, sabotage , which includes collab tracks “Right On Time” (with the iconic Tegan and Sara), “Feel Less” (with Lights), and “Dancing in a Dream” (with Rêve). He also continued his legendary pop up parties, culminating with a 5,000 person beach party in Vancouver and the first ever rave at the top of the CN Tower in Toronto. His club ready house tracks are cherished by fans across the spectrum of dance music.
location: UPSTAIRS ENTERTAINMENT PRESENTS : THE FREE LABEL, Upstairs Entertainment Ltd, 15 Bastion Square, Vic, Unknown, BC, Canada

event_name: Intro to Canadian Stepdance - French Canadian and Cape Breton Traditions
description: Discover the fun of French Canadian and Cape Breton stepdance with us live and in person! Intro to Canadian Stepdance Come tap your toes and move your feet to the fun music of Canada’s East Coast, and Québec! This will be an introduction to: (1) Cape Breton stepdance, from Nova Scotia, and (2) the seated “podorythmie” foot rhythms of Québec. All ages are welcome and no dance experience is necessary. Hard-soled shoes are recommended. Let’s dance and connect with the rich Canadian stepdance heritage together! About our instructor - Jocelyn Pettit From the West Coast of Canada, Jocelyn Pettit is a vibrant, award-winning fiddle player, stepdancer, singer, and educator. She has performed and taught across North America, the U.K., Europe and Malaysia; released five albums; and received multiple recognitions at the Canadian Folk Music Awards, Western Canadian Music Awards, and International Acoustic Music Awards. Jocelyn has a Master’s Degree in Traditional Music from the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland. As an educator, Jocelyn is passionate about sharing the joy and connection of music and dance. She has taught workshops at numerous schools and festivals around the world. Jocelyn is the founder of Coast Mountain Folk, celebrating folk and traditional music through community events in Squamish, BC.
location: 1774 16th AVE, VANCOUVER, BC V6J2M1, CA
